textByHash.40513756a7b0484d07b397d14b29b3817b198edf: Welcome to my discount camel store. Can I help you with anything?
textByHash.53c7fe8b5224f25d50ba91d7cc721f4b2c4fb653: Yes - it's my great idea - selling camels at discounted prices so that the common man can experience the joys of owning a camel of their very own! They're not just a source of kebab meat you know!
textByHash.e922a286d52078e233e53330debde391e7931c92: I would never sell one to the likes of you! They are beautiful, majestic creatures, not food! Get out of my shop.
textByHash.08c93761e6467366294e230898090f5da0ab0d06: Ah, wondrous camels! Best of all Saradomin's creations. Ships of the desert, far more useful than any horse or donkey. Truly, they are a man's best friend.
textByHash.3e55a91fd37571a989439bf23b54c4ba6ea7bee3: Perhaps you'd like a camel of your own? You're in the right place, if so...
textByHash.dd241fadb13f9f3ccf23fb54523268ea556e18a5: Is there anything else I can help you with?
textByHash.7a1a2c5fe47fd74d01a9fd43356235673c17d90c: Pah! I've seen mummies with more brains than you! If you treat camels badly then of course they're going to spit at you. May your robes never be clean again!
textByHash.cc6dba9b2c58b6acd18a874219840ca093bcbf8f: You're teasing me now! You rogue!
textByHash.105833d0fa1509b29baaea29cf5c55608523cc03: Everyone laughs at the poor Discount Camel Seller.
textByHash.6ea88e1c679092c67dae3f48fa2be197dc5abc7e: Business is booming! Pollnivneach has never seen this number of visitors.
textByHash.5ab0df358e0bfc4702e50fbcefb5499e27fec2ff: In fact I'm thinking of expanding and trying to break into the camel hire market.
textByHash.bcabb381abb1f352de400ad6436dcd4454fbc5ef: If only I could figure out how to get people to return the camels...
textByHash.05ce3b4c53f17449a5ac9042c24b675300664d34: Well, Pollnivneach is a funny little place. It's a small town and you'd think it'd be nice and quiet.
textByHash.aab7c0639438fa230908052d473501ed7124faab: But those Menaphites and bandits are making the place quite unbearable.
textByHash.a7ab4c90955d829e062bbb7ba5cb9b330f2b9e9d: Well, one group is as bad as the other! They steal, they fight, they cause trouble with us locals. Just yesterday one of those bandits came looking for protection money!
textByHash.86bf2246cab76608006d3dce397e60a748c59dd3: We're a small town. We don't have the resources to keep the gangs off our backs! Menaphos and Al Kharid are no help either.
textByHash.9b502d993e35dfdf89c4726d7f2149abf93565fd: To tell the truth we live in hope that an outsider, someone with no ties to either group, could broker a deal to halt the hostilities between them.
textByHash.3cd09b427525abb1da2a14a833b76357736b4f57: That's a little unfair, my friend. We can't all be brave adventurers. If we were, where would you purchase quality camels at discounted prices?
textByHash.f73ec8b274d4751baedc73c7431fa92a5b8a9bdc: You might think about that. Anyway, isn't it the job of the brave adventurer to protect the weak and innocent from evil and harm?
textByHash.7d52a67545c7e7ef9a3f35e06efd265a1f1c2cc2: Me?! I am but a humble discount camel seller. You're the adventurer, don't you know what to do?
textByHash.04ad318ffe233ecfaf4c2b53ea49573933716279: That is reassuring to hear. Too often one meets brainless adventurers whose solution to every problem is to hack, maim and murder.
textByHash.a4199fbd4b1cf7e64cf1e35c6a77f6233702245e: I'm glad to know you're not of that ilk.
textByHash.ca5383f46a863c8675f40bab20a69fc1d7192bd3: The state of adventurers today. They need everything spelled out for them.
textByHash.2a95d2c702307517107c26d1de93a8af97e24643: What, you want me to put a little note in your quest journal?
textByHash.7d50da4d46e4318dcbe72a389b4ac7ead2942bdb: But fine, I'll give you a hint. Try and see if you can get the gangs to agree to a truce by offering them each a token gift as an act of reconciliation.
textByHash.ea1970816a43a632346392d93a3f2f3ef0121cae: If that fails to work, you must consider trying to influence the gangs from within.
textByHash.c2dc3e9ad324b67997ba1395cfefbf79dea77e70: It's a beautiful day today, but word has it that it will rain tomorrow.
textByHash.6902ea90f4032bb2c3cbbb6e81ba9f033c4cd0b1: No! When have you ever known it to rain here in Gielinor?
textByHash.d39fb7e726c8911e7fda3a98e24c01490f807e06: Those two camels around the side are already sold.
textByHash.dea3d94e68ae831b32c049e20432b34b1872edc3: I've got new stock coming very soon though. Come back later if you're still interested.
textByHash.a970738a592319c32c9a8136e33d8eb8c43354d5: Why yes - all my uncles are called Ali!
textByHash.cfecc70985d4e01d1b817fc3414f931d546902b2: Well no, but one of them owns a moderately successful camel hire franchise.
textByHash.98eb25bc06ad0be26b82686dafcb8b17bad46d0e: Have you considered hiring a camel? Or perhaps purchasing one of your own? I can get you a discount.
textByHash.dd90c256af3da750b1176b600399d967a6154774: But you are in luck - I have another two beautiful camels arriving soon from the best stable in all of Al Kharid.
textByHash.628b0df282e6db8bebff16e2595d4dcd45778ee7: Well Sandy, the female camel, is a placid beast. At 20 years of age she comes from a prestigious line of camels - her mother was part of the Emir's baggage train!
textByHash.7807e0f0e312d1bd5c753357cf666b0c0ad5c962: Lumps, on the other hand, is a 15 year old male. He's a bit surly and prone to bouts of stubbornness. However, he more than makes up for this failing with his immense strength and stamina.
textByHash.7ed3a085ebd50065f070f81fa70efda10900146b: Both of them are of the 'Camelus Horribleus' family, a rare breed only found in this area of Gielinor. It is...
textByHash.da385ce51e36d4a09e436dce70f31201ce1f3a43: One cannot put a price on these beauties, they are precious beyond the worth of the crass value of gold coins!
textByHash.8127e36a4ebdb13e432601272f21a7cbc2095584: Are you trying to be funny?
textByHash.f5fde0668cf7c3e74e227e4d9cf2c7202fb4528b: Pah! That might buy you a mangy, flea-infested sheep if you're lucky. Don't insult me.
textByHash.1e98c488cc44c8f5de444e7f2df222965e9c22d2: I would consider giving them to you if you were able to look after them. But it is abundantly clear that you've got your hands - and your inventory - full at the moment.
textByHash.f0686d9e5fade0f1e14b99701ec92c20834abf4b: You don't even have that much money do you? Come back when you're able to make a proper offer and stop wasting my time.
textByHash.b0ec1e541e3fbe33e47323c328fc09e503fc13f1: I suppose I can silence my moral objections for that.
textByHash.2dd52672c448ae67df86579673008e40275535b1: Here are the receipts for the camels, so that you can pick them up as soon as I get them in.
textByHash.56103cdea3f224b93f8f3b87e52be66654110bc4: You are too generous. I would have sold them for half of that!
textByHash.8ca86565881e25002182db41e5457c64198e2cc3: Hello! Welcome back to my discount camel store. How are those camels getting along?
textByHash.01324df830baf495723e5107e5ca74e335e3fa8f: I think you're trying to con me into giving you an extra receipt.
textByHash.0ac04e9cfb758e7504cb503661c859973e06fd13: Come back to me when you're not so burdened.
textByHash.422147d411a60889b5bb819bfd8da1eb4785b3d8: Why? did they not please you?
textByHash.99753b926366120d2a853d25d7a057b925fd3102: Nooooo! Not Sandy and Lumps! You beast! Get out of my shop this instant!
textByHash.5a30f3a9df2dcf3eecd484b7405b18b2f48b24b2: That's very responsible of you. Too many people let their camels run feral through the desert, instead of sending them to happy homes.
textByHash.ff563dc0820c592a52c9035c282f796c5ce8b3af: You want to watch out for those feral camels, you know.
textByHash.794f2cb7d9902b1fe27c89e7efb6c8e3eb1c4f38: A noble purpose for two noble beasts! Did you succeed?
textByHash.31079c772a971457d0dea0acd6f01315e405ab0d: Well, I wish you the best of luck.
textByHash.b4a7062297961c59d31064d4d4b5d69885132ee5: Not much really, I just want to thank you for what you have done for this town. I might even stick around now that business is better!
textByHash.c098f6f1ad18a6672f55cfb5ecf182d08c3e522c: Well I've been debating whether I should diversify into camel rental, too. Although I do have all these cages lying around... do you think there's a market for discount jackals?
